title: salix × amoena
intro: Salix × amoena, also known as the Amora willow, is a hybrid plant derived from the cross between Salix alba (white willow) and Salix fragilis (crack willow). It is valued for its medicinal properties, particularly its ability to reduce inflammation and relieve pain, thanks to the presence of salicin, a compound similar to aspirin. The health benefits of Salix × amoena include its use in treating fever, rheumatism, and headaches, as well as its potential to aid in the management of menstrual cramps and other symptoms associated with PMS. Herbal preparations of Salix × amoena can be made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, which can be used to harness its medicinal properties and promote overall well-being.
